body: {"rationale":"The bead is mostly execution-ready: it has clear PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE sections, named verification tests, package-level test commands, explicit parent/deps, and enough context for an agent to act. It loses points because the title is not imperative/scope-focused, the root-cause section cites files without file:line grounding, and the labels are missing an explicit area facet required by the lint rubric.","score":5,"suggested_fixes":["Rewrite the title as an imperative, subsystem-scoped action, e.g. `Add dual identity authorization for forwarded mutations in server federation`.","Add file:line citations to the root-cause bullets in the description, or otherwise ground the current-state claims in concrete source locations.","Add an explicit area label such as `area:server` or `area:federation` so the label set covers phase, area, kind, and cross-reference facets."],"waivers_applied":[]}
